assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends using Release Channel + 'No Minor or Node Upgrades' Exclusion as the primary strategy and provides detailed configuration commands

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' exclusion and uses --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support flag, explaining it automatically renews when upgrading to new minor version

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud command for configuring maintenance windows with weekend timing (BYDAY=SA) and narrow 2-hour windows aligned with change freezes

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention cluster disruption budget or disruption intervals for controlling patch/upgrade frequency

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Stable channel for production and discusses Extended channel option for compliance requirements with 24-month versions, though doesn't detail cost structure

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response explicitly warns against using 'no upgrades' exclusions in the 'What NOT to Do' section and emphasizes that security patches should auto-apply

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response provides multi-environment strategy but doesn't mention rollout sequencing or cluster disruption budget for controlling upgrade order or patch frequency

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides comprehensive gcloud commands for setting exclusions (--add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades) and maintenance windows with specific parameters
